title: liriodendron tulipifera subsp. obtusilobum
intro: Liriodendron tulipifera subsp. obtusilobum, also known as the obtuse-lobed tulip tree, is a subspecies of the tulip tree (Liriodendron tulipifera) native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help with wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and combating infections. The health benefits of Liriodendron tulipifera subsp. obtusilobum include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Liriodendron tulipifera subsp. obtusilobum include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
